title: Sunday Market at Botanical Garden this weekend, but no plant sale
bodyText:
ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - The Garden Route Botanical Garden is holding another Sunday Market this weekend, on 8 June, but there will be no plant sale.

Their Autumn Plant Sale was held last weekend and was a huge success.


> Entry to the popular Sunday Market is free and it promises to be a vibrant and fun-filled day, starting at 10:00 and concluding at 14:00.


There will be a jumping castle and face painting for the children. Dogs on leashes are welcome.

Visitors can enjoy some delicious fare, from boerewors rolls, roosterkoek and pancakes to halal meals, Indian food, soups, jaffles, bubble tea and more.

Crafts and gifts stalls will offer a huge variety of local treasures, including plants, handmade clothes, soaps, jewellery, art, candles, gnomes, skincare and unique 3D-printed items.


> It’s a perfect outing for the whole family.


‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
